question.text: Under SEBI ICDR Regulations, what is the minimum promoter contribution required in a public issue?

answer.text: At least 10% of post-issue paid-up capital
At least 25% of post-issue paid-up capital
At least 20% of post-issue paid-up capital
At least 15% of post-issue paid-up capital

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI ICDR mandates that promoters must contribute at least 20% of the post-issue paid-up capital. If there is a shortfall, it can be made up by contributions from AIFs, FVCIs, banks, financial institutions, or insurers -- but each such entity can contribute a maximum of 10%. **The Trap:** 25% is the maximum buyback limit, not the promoter contribution requirement. 10% is the individual entity cap for shortfall contribution, not the promoter minimum. Getting these numbers crossed is a common exam error. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series II-A Chapter 7 covers promoter contribution requirements. Series IX covers due diligence on promoter contribution. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Promoter contribution of 20% is a skin-in-the-game requirement. If promoters struggle to meet even this minimum and need shortfall contributors, investigate why -- it could signal weak promoter financial capacity or lack of commitment.
